messages.content: Rewrite, the definition, Active screening meaning: ACF team or staff go to the village/communities or centre for the purpose of screening the nutritional status through MUAC, Weight, Height, Age, without waiting the beneficiary come to the team by self.

Active screening refers to the process in which the ACF team or staff proactively visit villages, communities or centers to assess the nutritional status of individuals using methods such as MUAC, weight, height, and age. This approach eliminates the need for beneficiaries to seek out the team themselves.
write an indicator definition for the indicator, "Number of children under five identified with undernutrition who are referred to ACF INCs"

The indicator "Number of children under five identified with undernutrition who are referred to ACF INCs" measures the volume of children under the age of five who have been identified as undernourished and have been referred to ACF INCs (Intensive Nutritional Care Centers) for further diagnosis and treatment. This indicator tracks the effectiveness and reach of the referral system in identifying and treating undernourished children, highlighting the progress made towards reducing undernutrition rates within the targeted population.

Write an indicator definition and calculation method, for the indicator;Number of PLWs (pregnant and lactating women and mothers of children under five who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices.

Indicator definition: 

The indicator "Number of pregnant and lactating women and mothers of children under five who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices" measures the reach and effectiveness of nutrition education programs that aim to improve the knowledge and practices of pregnant and lactating women, as well as mothers of children under five, in terms of nutrition and feeding practices.

Calculation method:

The number of pregnant and lactating women, as well as mothers of children under five, who have received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices can be calculated by adding up the number of women who have participated in any educational sessions, workshops, trainings or other interventions designed to improve knowledge and awareness about nutrition and feeding practices. 

For instance: 

(Number of pregnant women who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices) + (Number of lactating women who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices) + (Number of mothers of children under five who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices) = Total number of women who received knowledge on nutrition and feeding practices. 

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis, such as monthly, quarterly, or annually, to track the progress and effectiveness of nutrition education programs.
Write an Indicator definition and calculation method, for the indicator :” % of target population using over 20 litres of safe water per day”

Indicator definition:

The indicator "% of target population using over 20 litres of safe water per day" measures the proportion of the targeted population that has access to and uses a minimum amount of safe drinking water daily to maintain household and personal needs. The indicator aims to assess the sufficiency of safe water supply and usage among the target population.

Calculation method:

To calculate this indicator, the following formula can be used:

(Number of individuals in target population using over 20 litres of safe water per day ÷ Total number of individuals in target population) × 100

The numerator can be obtained by collecting data on the number of individuals within the target population that uses a minimum of 20 liters of safe water per day, this information can be obtained through household surveys, water testing, or other relevant data collection methods. 

For instance: If the total population of the area is 10,000 and the data collected showed that 6,000 individuals use over 20 liters of safe water per day, then the calculation will be:

(6,000 ÷ 10,000) × 100 = 60%

This means that 60% of the target population is using over 20 litres of safe water daily. 

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis, such as monthly, quarterly, or annually, to track the progress and effectiveness of water supply and sanitation programs within the targeted area.
Write an Indicator definition and calculation method, for the indicator :” % of installed water points in good working order 6 month after handover to the village WASH committee”

Indicator definition:

The indicator "% of installed water points in good working order 6 months after handover to the village WASH committee" measures the percentage of installed water points or supplies that remain functional and in good working condition six months after being handed over to the village's Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH) committee. The indicator aims to assess the management and maintenance capacity of the WASH committee in sustaining the installed water points over the long term.

Calculation method:

To calculate this indicator, the following formula can be used:

(Number of functional water points six months after handover ÷ Total number of water points handed over) × 100

The numerator can be obtained through visual inspections, water quality testing, and/or user feedback by the WASH committee or other relevant stakeholders on the functionality of the water points six months following the handover to the WASH committee. 

For instance: If 30 water points were handed over to the WASH committee, and after six months, 25 are found to be in good working order, then the calculation will be:

(25 ÷ 30) × 100 = 83.33%

Thus, the percentage of installed water points in good working order six months after handover is 83.33%.

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis, such as every six months or annually, to monitor the maintenance and management capacity of the WASH committee in sustaining water supplies over time.

rewrite, "Dear Nyein, Let me gentle follow up on this matter. if it is not convenient to get the approval from Senior Management Team, it is okay that HR lead can verified/apporved in the requested working certificate. "

Dear Nyein, 
I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to kindly follow up on the matter we previously discussed. If it is not possible to obtain approval from the Senior Management Team, it is acceptable for the HR lead to verify and approve the requested working certificate. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. 
Best regards,
write an letter for submitting CV and application letter for applying MERL manger

[Your Name]
[Your Address]
[City, State ZIP code]
[Your Email]
[Date]

[Recipient's Name]
[Recipient's Address]
[City, State ZIP code]
[Recipient's Email]

Subject: Application for MERL Manager role

Dear [Recipient's Name],

I am writing to submit my application for the MERL Manager role at [Organization's Name], as advertised on [Source of Job Posting]. As an experienced Monitoring, Evaluation, Research and Learning professional with [Number of Years] years of experience, I believe my qualifications, skills, and expertise are a perfect match for this role.

I hold a [Degree and Major] from [University], and I am equipped with a diverse skill set, including the ability to design and implement MERL systems, provide technical support to project teams, conduct data analysis, and develop evidence-based reports. My experience in leading MERL programs, managing project cycles, and supporting capacity-building initiatives in international development contexts has provided me with a solid foundation to excel in this position.

I am confident that my proficiency in Results-Based Monitoring and Evaluation, Qualitative and Quantitative Research methods, Data Management and Analysis, and Learning and Knowledge Management will be of great value to [Organization's Name].

Attached to this email, please find my resume and application letter highlighting my achievements and qualifications. I would appreciate the opportunity to discuss my application in more detail and learn about the [Organization's Name] initiatives and aspirations. 

Thank you for considering my application. I am looking forward to hearing back from you.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
write an definitions and indicator calculation for the indicator ,"% of target population using adequate or healthy latrines "

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "% of target population using adequate or healthy latrines" measures the proportion of the targeted population that has access to and uses a healthy or adequate latrine facility that fulfills basic hygiene requirements, ultimately reducing the likelihood of water and sanitation-borne illnesses.

Calculation method:

To calculate this indicator, the following formula can be used:

(Number of individuals in the target population using healthy or adequate latrines ÷ Total number of individuals in the target population) × 100

The numerator can be determined through household surveys, field observations, or other relevant data collection methods to identify the portion of individuals within the target population using a latrine that is appropriate for human waste disposal, while fulfilling the necessary hygiene requirements. 

For example, if a household survey was conducted in an area with a target population of 1,000 people, and 800 people were found to be using healthy or adequate latrines, the calculation for the indicator would be:

(800 ÷ 1,000) × 100 = 80%

Thus, 80% of the target population is using adequate or healthy latrines. 

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis, such as monthly, quarterly, or annually, to track progress towards improving access to and use of appropriate sanitation facilities for the targeted population.
what does mean "adequate and healthy latrine"

An adequate and healthy latrine is a sanitation facility that fulfills basic hygiene requirements and provides a safe and private space for human waste disposal. 

An adequate latrine should have enough capacity to contain and manage the amount of waste generated, ensuring that the pit or container does not fill up prior to the scheduled maintenance. Additionally, an adequate latrine should be constructed to prevent groundwater contamination and withstand natural disasters that may cause damage.

A healthy latrine, on the other hand, should be well-maintained, clean, properly ventilated, and free of odors, insects, and rodents. This is important to reduce the transmission of diseases and promote hygienic behavior.

Overall, adequate and healthy latrines contribute to protecting public health by preventing the spread of diseases and contamination of the environment.
write an indicator definition and calculation for the indicator, "% of children under 5 who in the past two weeks had at least 3 loose or liquid stools in one day"

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "% of children under 5 who, in the past two weeks, had at least 3 loose or liquid stools in one day" measures the proportion of children under the age of five in a given population who have experienced diarrhea, as it is a key morbidity indicator for child health and nutrition.

Calculation method:

To calculate this indicator, the following formula can be used:

(Number of children under 5 who had at least 3 loose or liquid stools in one day in the past two weeks ÷ Total number of children under 5 in the target population) × 100

The numerator can be obtained through household surveys, routine monitoring, or other relevant data collection methods to identify the number of children under five in a given population who experienced diarrhea in the past two weeks.

For example, if a household survey identified that in a population of 500 children under 5 years of age, 60 children had 3 loose or liquid stools in one day in the past two weeks, the calculation for the indicator would be:

(60 ÷ 500) × 100 = 12%

Thus, 12% of children under 5 in the target population had at least 3 loose or liquid stools in one day in the past two weeks.

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis such as monthly, quarterly or annually. The data collected can be used to highlight the need for interventions to reduce the incidence of diarrhea in children under five within the target population.
write an indicator definition and calculation, for the indicator"Number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW benefitting from at least one care practices and/or mental health session."

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "Number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW benefiting from at least one care practices and/or mental health session" measures the number of caregivers, including parents or guardians of children under the age of five, and Pregnant and Lactating Women (PLW), who have attended at least one session focused on care practices or mental health support.

Calculation Method:

The number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW who benefit from at least one care practice and/or mental health session can be calculated by adding the number of individuals who have attended at least one session designed to provide care practices or mental health support.

For example, if 500 caregivers of children under five and PLW were invited to attend care practice and mental health sessions, and 400 attend at least one session, then the calculation would be:

Total number of caregivers benefiting from at least one care practices and/or mental health session = 400

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis such as monthly, quarterly or annually to monitor the progression and effectiveness of care practice and/or mental health sessions in improving the health and well-being of caregivers of children under five and PLW.

This indicator can be used to identify the needs and gaps in the care practice and mental health support available to the targeted population, identify the effectiveness of the sessions, and improve the design of interventions to meet the specific needs of the caregivers.

write an indicator definition and calculation, for the indicator"Number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW reporting improved mental health and psychosocial wellbeing and care practices as a result of services."

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "Number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW reporting improved mental health and psychosocial wellbeing and care practices as a result of services" measures the number of caregivers, including parents or guardians of children under the age of five, and Pregnant and Lactating Women (PLW), who report improved mental health and psychosocial wellbeing and care practices as a result of services received.

Calculation Method:

The number of caregivers of children under 5 and PLW reporting improved mental health and psychosocial wellbeing and care practices as a result of services can be calculated by collecting data on the number of individuals who report experiencing improvements in their mental health and psychosocial wellbeing, as well as their care practices, post-services received.

For instance, if 200 caregivers of children under 5 and PLW received services that aim to enhance their mental health, psychosocial wellbeing, and care practices, and 150 caregivers report experiencing improvements, the calculation would be:

Total number of caregivers reporting improved mental health, psychosocial wellbeing, and care practices = 150

This indicator can be reported on a periodic basis such as monthly, quarterly or annually, to monitor the effectiveness and impact of mental health and psychosocial support interventions for caregivers of children under 5 and PLW. 

This indicator can be used to evaluate services and interventions designed to enable caregivers to support the physical, mental, and psychosocial well-being of their children and themselves, identify areas for improvement, and guide decision-making regarding program design and delivery.
write an indicator definition and calculation, for the indicator "Number of people in need of protection assistance supported with psychosocial support in Community Care Spaces

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "Number of people in need of protection assistance supported with psychosocial support in Community Care Spaces" measures the number of individuals who have received psychosocial support services within Community Care Spaces (CCS) to address protection concerns, promote psychosocial wellbeing, and enhance coping mechanisms.

Calculation Method:

The number of people in need of protection assistance who received psychosocial support in Community Care Spaces can be calculated by collecting data on the total number of individuals who have received psychosocial support services in CCS to address their specific needs and concerns.

For example, if 300 individuals in a vulnerable population were assessed as needing protection assistance, accessing CCS services, and participating in relevant interventions, and 200 received psychosocial support services, then the calculation would be:

Number of people supported with psychosocial support in Community Care Spaces = 200

This indicator can be reported periodically (monthly, quarterly, or annually) and tracked over time to monitor the extent to which psychosocial support is being provided to those in need of protection assistance in CCS. 

This indicator can be used to evaluate the effectiveness of protective services and programs, identify the gaps and requirements of those currently receiving care, and plan interventions to improve access to psychosocial support, as well as overall well-being, of individuals in need of protection assistance.

write and indicator definition and calculation, for the indicator "Number of people in need of protection assistance supported with referrals"

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "Number of people in need of protection assistance supported with referrals" measures the number of individuals who have received referrals to other specialized services or providers as a part of their protection assistance program from their initial assessment, referral, and follow-up.

Calculation Method:

The number of people in need of protection assistance and supported with referrals can be calculated by adding up the total number of individuals who have received referrals to other specialized services or providers as a part of their protection assistance program.

For instance, if 500 individuals in a vulnerable population were assessed as needing protection assistance, and 300 received a referral to specialized services such as legal aid, healthcare, shelter, or other support providers during a specified period of time, then the calculation would be:

Number of people supported with referrals = 300

This indicator can be reported periodically (monthly, quarterly, or annually) and tracked over time to monitor the extent to which individuals in need of protection assistance are successfully being referred to required specialized services. 

This indicator can be used to evaluate the effectiveness of the protective services and programs, identify the gaps and requirements of those currently receiving care, and plan interventions to improve the referral pathways for individuals in need of protection assistance.
write an indicator definition and calculation, for the indicator,Number of people participating in awareness raising with protection key messages

Indicator Definition:

The indicator "Number of people participating in awareness raising with protection key messages" measures the number of individuals who have participated in awareness-raising activities aimed at promoting protection key messages, such as the identification of protection risks, referral mechanisms, fundamental human rights, legal frameworks, and access to protection assistance.

Calculation Method:

The number of people participating in awareness raising with protection key messages can be calculated by collecting data on the total number of individuals who have participated in awareness-raising activities related to protection key messages.

For example, if 1,000 individuals participated in a community meeting promoting protection key messages related to sexual exploitation and abuse during a specified period of time, then the calculation for the indicator would be:

Number of people participating in awareness-raising with protection key messages = 1,000
